text.title: backup-verification-dr Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for backup-verification-dr as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent creates a restore-drill subagent that restores to the production database or namespace because the test environment is "not available".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A faulty drill can overwrite live data. Restore drills must always isolate scratch resources: separate namespace, separate AWS account if possible. If the test environment is not available, block the drill and alert — do not fall back to production.

failure-mode.description: Agent monitors only backup_last_status (success/failure) and misses silent data corruption, empty backups, and wrong-path dumps.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Monitor backup_last_success_timestamp (age), backup_size_bytes (delta), and backup_duration_seconds (regression). A successful empty backup is a critical failure that shows up only in size monitoring.

failure-mode.description: Agent runs `restic forget --prune` autonomously without a dry-run gate or human approval.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Forget/prune is irreversible. Agent may emit `forget --dry-run` only; a human runs the real prune after reviewing the dry-run output. Add a guardrail to any agentic workflow that touches retention operations.
